meta.title: The Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: A deterministic end-of-day reflection tool traversing three psychological axes: Locus, Orientation, and Radius.
meta.author: DT Fellowship Candidate

nodes.text:
Good evening. You made it to the end of the day.

This is a short conversation — just for you. No scores, no records, no judgments.

We'll walk through three simple questions about today. Take your time.
Before we dive in — how would you describe today in one word?

options.label: Productive
options.value: Productive

options.label: Frustrating
options.value: Frustrating

options.label: Draining
options.value: Draining

options.label: Steady
options.value: Steady

options.label: Chaotic
options.value: Chaotic

nodes.text:
You said "{A0_OPEN.answer}" — sounds like you found some footing today.

When something went well, what made it happen?

options.label: I had prepared and it paid off
options.value: I had prepared and it paid off

options.label: I adapted quickly when the plan changed
options.value: I adapted quickly when the plan changed

options.label: The right people showed up
options.value: The right people showed up

options.label: Honestly, a bit of luck
options.value: Honestly, a bit of luck

nodes.text:
You said "{A0_OPEN.answer}" — those days happen.

When things got difficult today, what was your first instinct?

options.label: Figure out what I could still control
options.value: Figure out what I could still control

options.label: Push through on my own
options.value: Push through on my own

options.label: Wait to see if someone else would handle it
options.value: Wait to see if someone else would handle it

options.label: Feel stuck — it wasn't really in my hands
options.value: Feel stuck — it wasn't really in my hands

nodes.text: Think about a moment today when something didn't go as planned. What did you do with it?

options.label: I asked myself what I could do differently
options.value: I asked myself what I could do differently

options.label: I found a workaround and kept moving
options.value: I found a workaround and kept moving

options.label: I felt frustrated by circumstances outside my control
options.value: I felt frustrated by circumstances outside my control

options.label: I needed someone else to step in before I could move forward
options.value: I needed someone else to step in before I could move forward

nodes.text:
You see your hand in what happened today.

That's not about blame — it's about agency. You stayed in the driver's seat, even when the road got rough. That's a choice, and you made it.

Notice that.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
Hard days pull your attention outward — to what others did or didn't do, to systems that didn't work, to luck that didn't show up.

That's human. But somewhere in there, you made a call too. Even choosing to wait is a choice. Even feeling stuck is a response.

You had more authorship today than you might think.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
We've been looking at how you navigated today.

Now let's shift — from how you moved through the day, to what you put into it.
Think about one interaction you had today — a meeting, a message, a quick hallway exchange. Which of these best describes it?

options.label: I helped someone with something that wasn't strictly my job
options.value: I helped someone with something that wasn't strictly my job

options.label: I taught someone something, or shared what I knew
options.value: I taught someone something, or shared what I knew

options.label: I felt I deserved more recognition than I got
options.value: I felt I deserved more recognition than I got

options.label: I felt frustrated that others weren't holding up their end
options.value: I felt frustrated that others weren't holding up their end

nodes.text: At the end of today, which thought crossed your mind more?

options.label: "Did I give enough today?"
options.value: Did I give enough today?

options.label: "Did I do enough extra to be noticed?"
options.value: Did I do enough extra to be noticed?

options.label: "What did the team get out of today?"
options.value: What did the team get out of today?

options.label: "Did I get credit for what I contributed?"
options.value: Did I get credit for what I contributed?

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you went beyond what was asked of you — without expecting anything back?

options.label: Yes — and it felt natural
options.value: Yes — and it felt natural

options.label: Yes — but I noticed I was hoping someone would see it
options.value: Yes — but I noticed I was hoping someone would see it

options.label: Not really — I stuck to what was expected
options.value: Not really — I stuck to what was expected

options.label: I wanted to, but didn't get the chance
options.value: I wanted to, but didn't get the chance

nodes.text:
Today, your attention went outward.

You thought about what you could give — not just what you'd receive. That's not a small thing. Most people run the other calculation without noticing.

Contribution doesn't require grand gestures. What you did today counts.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
It's easy — and human — to keep score.

When we've worked hard, we notice what we're owed. That's not weakness. But it can quietly shrink your experience of work.

Tomorrow: try giving one thing with no expectation of return. Notice how that feels different.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
We've looked at how you responded, and what you gave.

One last shift — from what today meant to *you*, to what it meant to the people around you.
Think about today's biggest challenge or moment of effort. Who comes to mind when you think about it?

options.label: Just me — it was my problem to solve
options.value: Just me — it was my problem to solve

options.label: My team — we were all in it together
options.value: My team — we were all in it together

options.label: A specific colleague who was struggling more than me
options.value: A specific colleague who was struggling more than me

options.label: The person or team who would be affected by the outcome
options.value: The person or team who would be affected by the outcome

nodes.text: Did you check in with anyone today — not for a task update, but just to see how they were doing?

options.label: Yes, and it was meaningful
options.value: Yes, and it was meaningful

options.label: I thought about it but didn't get to it
options.value: I thought about it but didn't get to it

options.label: No — I was too focused on my own work
options.value: No — I was too focused on my own work

options.label: Someone checked in with me, actually
options.value: Someone checked in with me, actually

nodes.text: When you picture the impact of your work today — where does your mind go?

options.label: My own deliverables and goals
options.value: My own deliverables and goals

options.label: The team's overall outcome
options.value: The team's overall outcome

options.label: The customer or end user we're ultimately serving
options.value: The customer or end user we're ultimately serving

options.label: I don't think about it much, honestly
options.value: I don't think about it much, honestly

nodes.text:
Today, your radius extended beyond yourself.

You thought about the team. The colleague. The person at the other end of your work.

That widening is not a soft skill — it's the source of the most durable motivation at work. You're not just doing a job. You're part of something.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
Today, your attention stayed close to home — your work, your goals, your stress.

There's nothing wrong with that. But sometimes the best antidote to a hard day isn't more focus on yourself — it's realizing someone nearby had it just as hard.

Tomorrow: look one person wider. You might find the day feels different.

options.label: Continue
options.value: continue

nodes.text:
Here's what today looked like, through three lenses:

**Agency:** You leaned {axis1.dominant} today — {axis1.summary}.

**Contribution:** You leaned {axis2.dominant} today — {axis2.summary}.

**Radius:** You leaned {axis3.dominant} today — {axis3.summary}.

{closing_insight}

interpolations.axis1.summary_internal: you saw your hand in what happened
interpolations.axis1.summary_external: circumstances felt bigger than your choices
interpolations.axis2.summary_contribution: you thought about what you could give
interpolations.axis2.summary_entitlement: you noticed what you were owed
interpolations.axis3.summary_self: your focus stayed on your own work

closing_insights.internal_contribution_altrocentric: Three pillars. That's a strong day — even if it didn't feel like it.
closing_insights.internal_contribution_self: You acted and gave — now try widening to the people around you.
closing_insights.internal_entitlement_altrocentric: You owned your actions and thought of others. The contribution piece is worth revisiting.
closing_insights.internal_entitlement_self: You had agency today. The next step is turning that agency outward.
closing_insights.external_contribution_altrocentric: You gave and cared — even without feeling fully in control. That's underrated.
closing_insights.external_contribution_self: You gave today. Anchor that. And tomorrow, notice one small choice that was yours.
closing_insights.external_entitlement_self: Today felt like it happened to you. That's real. But notice: you still made calls. Start there.
closing_insights.external_entitlement_altrocentric: Mixed signals today — you reached out to others but felt unrecognized. Both can be true. Both are worth sitting with.

options.label: Close session
options.value: close

nodes.text: See you tomorrow.
